findings.description: `assistant run` bypasses `DefaultToolPolicy` entirely: `_run_repl_with_registry` passes `registry.list_all()` directly to `adapter.create_agent`, so `role.preferred_tools` is not applied to HTTP tools and every discovered operation is exposed to every role. The same unfiltered `http_tools` list is reused after `/role` switches and delegation. See src/assistant/cli.py:311-322 and src/assistant/cli.py:353-369.
findings.resolution: Route SDK startup through `CapabilityResolver(http_tool_registry=registry)` and `capabilities.tools.authorized_tools(...)`, or otherwise filter HTTP tools with the same exact-name semantics before creating each agent. Recompute the authorized tool list on role switch and delegation.

findings.description: Calling public `discover_tools(tool_sources)` without an injected client returns tools that close over a client that has already been closed in the `finally` block. Discovery succeeds, but any later invocation of a returned tool will fail because `_build_tool` captured the closed `httpx.AsyncClient`. See src/assistant/http_tools/discovery.py:120-135 and src/assistant/http_tools/builder.py:191-232.
findings.resolution: Do not close an owned client before returned tools can be invoked. Either require caller-managed client lifecycle, make `HttpToolRegistry` own an async closeable client, or return tools that create/use a live client at invocation time with explicit lifecycle semantics.

findings.description: `parse_operations` catches all `_parse_operation` `ValueError`s and logs from `openapi.py`, so `discover_tools` never sees external or cyclic `$ref` failures. This violates the external-ref scenario requiring a warning naming the source plus method/path, and the cyclic-ref scenario requiring a source-level skip surfaced through discovery. See src/assistant/http_tools/openapi.py:56-60 and src/assistant/http_tools/discovery.py:189-193.
findings.resolution: Distinguish operation-skippable external refs from source-fatal cyclic refs, and emit discovery warnings with source context. At minimum, propagate enough error metadata from the parser so discovery can log `source + method/path`; cyclic refs should raise out to `_discover_one` and omit the source.

findings.description: `export_tool_manifest` emits `http_tools` as a list of objects like `{"name": ..., "description": ...}`, but the tool-policy spec requires a list of registered HTTP tool keys as strings. The test codifies the wrong shape by extracting `e["name"]` instead of asserting the list equals the keys. See src/assistant/core/capabilities/tools.py:69-72 and tests/core/capabilities/test_tool_policy_http.py:184-190.
findings.resolution: Return `"http_tools": [t.name, ...]` in registry order, or update the spec if the richer object shape is intentional. Align the test to assert the exact contract shape.

findings.description: `CapabilityResolver` accepts `http_tool_registry`, but only passes it into `DefaultToolPolicy` for `harness_type == "host"`; the SDK/default path constructs `DefaultToolPolicy()` without the registry. Any SDK consumer using the resolver gets no HTTP tools from policy resolution. See src/assistant/core/capabilities/resolver.py:110-114.
findings.resolution: Pass `http_tool_registry=self._http_tool_registry` to `DefaultToolPolicy` in the non-host path as well, unless a custom `tool_factory` is provided.

findings.description: The per-tool content-type gate allows a non-empty 2xx body with no `Content-Type` header to be parsed as JSON, even though the spec says non-JSON 2xx content types must raise. It also rejects common JSON-compatible media types such as `application/vnd.api+json`, despite the spec allowing JSON-compatible variants. See src/assistant/http_tools/builder.py:246-254.
findings.resolution: Require a JSON content type for non-empty bodies, and accept `application/json`, `application/problem+json`, and media types ending in `+json`. Keep the existing 204 and `Content-Length: 0` empty-body bypass before the gate.

findings.description: The OpenAPI walker ignores path-item-level `parameters` and only reads `operation.get("parameters")`. Valid OpenAPI documents commonly declare path parameters at the path-item level, which means `_build_tool` will not mark them as path params and `{id}` placeholders will not be substituted. See src/assistant/http_tools/openapi.py:49-57 and src/assistant/http_tools/openapi.py:72-77.
findings.resolution: Merge `path_item.get("parameters", [])` with operation-level parameters before resolution, with operation-level entries overriding duplicates by `(in, name)`. Add a test where `/items/{id}` declares `id` on the path item.

findings.description: Object request bodies using `additionalProperties` are silently unusable. `_build_args_schema` only exposes named `properties`, so a schema like `{type: object, additionalProperties: {type: string}}` produces an empty args model; supplied dynamic keys are ignored and no JSON body is sent. See src/assistant/http_tools/builder.py:119-130 and src/assistant/http_tools/builder.py:160-164.
findings.resolution: For object schemas without named properties but with `additionalProperties`, expose a body field typed as `dict[str, value_type]` or configure a generated model that preserves extra keys and sends them as the JSON body.
